    base ¶

    Classes:

    • BinaryDistribution –

      A probability distribution for discrete values.

    • ContinuousDistribution –

      A probability distribution for continuous values.

    • DiscreteDistribution –

      A probability distribution for discrete values.

    • Distribution –

      General distribution.

    BinaryDistribution ¶

    BinaryDistribution(seed: int | None = None)

    Bases: Distribution

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.proba.base.BinaryDistribution[BinaryDistribution]
              vendor.river.river.proba.base.Distribution[Distribution]

                              vendor.river.river.proba.base.Distribution --> vendor.river.river.proba.base.BinaryDistribution
                


              click vendor.river.river.proba.base.BinaryDistribution href "" "vendor.river.river.proba.base.BinaryDistribution"
              click vendor.river.river.proba.base.Distribution href "" "vendor.river.river.proba.base.Distribution"

    A probability distribution for discrete values.

    Parameters¶

    seed Random number generator seed for reproducibility.

    Methods:

    • revert –

      Reverts the parameters of the distribution for a given observation.

    • sample –

      Sample a random value from the distribution.

    • update –

      Updates the parameters of the distribution given a new observation.

    Attributes:

    • mode –

      The most likely value in the distribution.

    • n_samples –

      The number of observed samples.

    mode abstractmethod property ¶

    mode

    The most likely value in the distribution.

    n_samples abstractmethod property ¶

    n_samples

    The number of observed samples.

    revert abstractmethod ¶

    revert(x: bool) -> None

    Reverts the parameters of the distribution for a given observation.

    sample abstractmethod ¶

    sample()

    Sample a random value from the distribution.

    update abstractmethod ¶

    update(x: bool) -> None

    Updates the parameters of the distribution given a new observation.

    ContinuousDistribution ¶

    ContinuousDistribution(seed: int | None = None)

    Bases: Distribution

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.proba.base.ContinuousDistribution[ContinuousDistribution]
              vendor.river.river.proba.base.Distribution[Distribution]

                              vendor.river.river.proba.base.Distribution --> vendor.river.river.proba.base.ContinuousDistribution
                


              click vendor.river.river.proba.base.ContinuousDistribution href "" "vendor.river.river.proba.base.ContinuousDistribution"
              click vendor.river.river.proba.base.Distribution href "" "vendor.river.river.proba.base.Distribution"

    A probability distribution for continuous values.

    Parameters¶

    seed Random number generator seed for reproducibility.

    Methods:

    • cdf –

      Cumulative density function, i.e. P(X <= x).

    • revert –

      Reverts the parameters of the distribution for a given observation.

    • sample –

      Sample a random value from the distribution.

    • update –

      Updates the parameters of the distribution given a new observation.

    Attributes:

    • mode –

      The most likely value in the distribution.

    • n_samples –

      The number of observed samples.

    mode abstractmethod property ¶

    mode

    The most likely value in the distribution.

    n_samples abstractmethod property ¶

    n_samples

    The number of observed samples.

    cdf abstractmethod ¶

    cdf(x: float)

    Cumulative density function, i.e. P(X <= x).

    revert abstractmethod ¶

    revert(x: float) -> None

    Reverts the parameters of the distribution for a given observation.

    sample abstractmethod ¶

    sample()

    Sample a random value from the distribution.

    update abstractmethod ¶

    update(x: float) -> None

    Updates the parameters of the distribution given a new observation.

    • update –

      The most likely value in the distribution.

              click vendor.river.river.proba.base.Distribution href "" "vendor.river.river.proba.base.Distribution"

    A probability distribution for multivariate continuous values.

    Parameters¶

    seed Random number generator seed for reproducibility.

    Methods:

    • cdf –

      Cumulative density function, i.e. P(X <= x).

    • revert –

      Reverts the parameters of the distribution for a given observation.

    • sample –

      Sample a random value from the distribution.

    • update –

      Updates the parameters of the distribution given a new observation.

    Attributes:

    • mode –

      The most likely value in the distribution.

    • n_samples –

      The number of observed samples.

    mode abstractmethod property ¶

    mode

    The most likely value in the distribution.

    n_samples abstractmethod property ¶

    n_samples

    The number of observed samples.

    cdf abstractmethod ¶

    cdf(x: dict[str, float]) -> float

    Cumulative density function, i.e. P(X <= x).

    revert abstractmethod ¶

    revert(x: dict[str, float]) -> None

    Reverts the parameters of the distribution for a given observation.

    sample abstractmethod ¶

    sample()

    Sample a random value from the distribution.

    update abstractmethod ¶

    update(x: dict[str, float]) -> None

    Updates the parameters of the distribution given a new observation.
